wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: A sailboat in the British Virgin Islands
A lobster boat off the coast of Maine
The Green River valley on the Coal Pick property wrapped in morning light
A sweeping view of the 4,200-acre Coal Pick property — the Green River valley, hardwood ridges, and open fields in autumn
The Coal Pick Outdoors property in Kentucky
The host of Coal Pick Outdoors walking a Kentucky field above the river valley
Sydney Shrewsberry, host of Coal Pick Outdoors
An American bison grazing in a tallgrass meadow
A mature whitetail buck in a green clearing
A bull elk in a sunlit autumn meadow
River bottom and hardwood ridge. Elk, bison, and whitetail. This is the home ground behind Coal Pick — and the start of every adventure.

Tucked into the Green River bottom of Muhlenberg County, the Coal Pick property runs 4,200 acres of managed hardwood forest, open field, and water — one of the top Boone & Crockett counties in the nation.
White oak, red oak, hickory, black walnut, and cedar stands give way to more than twenty-five lakes and swamps. It's been under private herd management for two decades, and it shows in every season.
The Green River bottom at first light · Paradise, KY
Elk bugling in the fall. Bison on the tallgrass. Whitetail, turkey, waterfowl, quail, and bass in twenty-five waters. This is a working wildlife refuge as much as it is a hunting ground.

Sydney Shrewsberry is the one taking you across the land and out on the road — Miss Indiana 2025, marketing degree in hand, equally at home on a hunting stand or the bow of a fishing boat.

Each episode of the series follows her onto the property and beyond it, chasing the seasons and the adventures behind the brand.
Twelve episodes a year. From the home ground in Kentucky to wild country coast to coast.
A season on the land: elk, bison, and the Green River bottom.
Out on the Atlantic hauling traps off the ocean floor.
Chasing the silver king on the Gulf Coast flats.
Island to island across the Caribbean.
A ghost town, a river, and the ground beneath it.

Paradise, Kentucky isn't on most maps anymore — but it's on ours. In 1820, the first commercial coal mine in America opened right here in Muhlenberg County. A few miles up the Green River, a Scottish lord built the Airdrie Ironworks in 1855. This county was once called the Saudi Arabia of coal.

That's the ground Coal Pick sits on. Coal Pick Outdoors is how we share it — the land, the animals, and the adventures that come with them. When you're ready for the bottle, the distillery is one click away.
New episodes, behind-the-scenes footage, and first looks at where Coal Pick heads next.
